title: AppLovin Just Joined the S&P 500. Here's What History Suggests the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Do Next.
author: Jon Quast
description: AppLovin, an adtech company, was added to the S&P 500 in September 2025 after significant growth driven by its Axon 2.0 AI software. While S&P 500 inclusion doesn't guarantee future performance, the company shows promising growth potential by expanding beyond mobile gaming into e-commerce and connected TV advertising.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong revenue growth (68% in Q3 2025), expanding market reach, high-margin software, successful Axon 2.0 launch, and potential for growth in new market segments like e-commerce and connected TV
Mentioned as an example of a stock that performed well after S&P 500 inclusion, with over 36% growth since 2020

title: Why Tyler Technologies Stock Eked out a Gain on Thursday
author: Eric Volkman
description: Tyler Technologies, a public-sector-focused enterprise software company, saw its stock price gain slightly after being named a 'leader' and 'visionary' in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud-Based ERP for U.S. Local Government, a prestigious industry recognition.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article reports that Tyler Technologies' stock price gained slightly after the company was recognized as a 'leader' and 'visionary' in the Gartner Magic Quadrant, a prestigious industry designation. This positive recognition is seen as a boost to the company's reputation and standing in the market.

The article highlights Gartner's status as a veteran and influential tech industry researcher, and its Magic Quadrant as a prestigious industry recognition. Tyler Technologies' placement in the 'leader' and 'visionary' categories is seen as a positive for the company.

title: Why Tyler Technologies Stock Slipped on Monday
author: Eric Volkman
description: Tyler Technologies, a financial services company, saw its share price decline slightly after an analyst at Wells Fargo cut the price target, citing unclear reasons. However, the company's recent earnings report showed strong growth, and the author believes the company is fundamentally solid and attractively priced.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that Tyler Technologies is fundamentally solid and attractively priced, despite the analyst price target cut. The company's recent earnings report showed strong growth, and the author believes investors should be more bullish on the company's potential.

title: UnitedHealth, T-Mobile And Fiserv Are Among Top 12 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (Apr 21-Apr 25): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Several large-cap stocks, including Fiserv, UnitedHealth, and T-Mobile, were among the top losers last week. The declines were primarily due to factors such as weaker-than-expected financial results, guidance revisions, and analyst price target changes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fiserv shares tumbled after the company's FY25 adjusted EPS guidance midpoint fell below analysts' expectations, leading to price revisions.
UnitedHealth shares decreased after the company reported disappointing first-quarter results, and analysts revised the price forecast.
T-Mobile shares declined by 11.4% last week, making it one of the top 12 large-cap losers.
Northrop Grumman shares dipped after the company reported worse-than-expected results and lowered its FY25 EPS guidance, leading to several analysts lowering their price forecasts.
Erie Indemnity stock plummeted after the company reported financial results and missed its EPS estimate.
Watsco shares dipped after the company reported first-quarter results and missed its EPS and sales estimates, leading to analysts revising the price forecast.

Gold Fields shares decreased, likely due to a drop in gold prices after comments from President Trump about tariffs and the Federal Reserve.
Aon stock fell after the company reported financial results and missed its adjusted EPS and revenue estimates.
AngloGold Ashanti shares fell, likely due to the drop in gold prices after comments from President Trump about tariffs and the Federal Reserve.
Ryan Specialty Holdings stock was down after the company announced a deal to acquire certain assets of USQRisk Holdings.
Tyler Technologies stock fell after the company reported first-quarter results, and several analysts lowered their price forecasts.
Kimberly-Clark stock declined after the company reported weaker-than-expected first-quarter sales, and several analysts slashed their price forecast.
